sections.heading: Atlas Limits
sections.body:
Atlas limits the number of the following items:

- Components of particular type that you can create.

- Connections allowed to a cluster. For
  clusters, the number of connections allowed depends on the
  cluster tier.

- Global Cluster shard keys per [project](projects). Each
  Atlas project supports up to 40 Global Cluster shard keys.
  If you require multiple Global Clusters and might exceed
  this limit, deploy each additional Global Cluster in a
  separate project.

To learn more, see [Atlas Service Limits](atlas-limits). For questions or assistance, contact support:

sections.heading: Atlas Flex Cluster Limits
sections.body: Flex clusters have certain [supported for Flex clusters](flex-limits-config), [operational limits](flex-limits-ops), and [driver requirements](flex-limits-drivers). To learn more, see [Atlas Flex Limitations](flex-cluster-limitations).

sections.heading: Atlas Free Cluster Limits
sections.body: Free clusters (formerly known as `M0`) have certain [Configuration Limits](shared-limits-config), [operational limits](shared-limits-ops), and [driver requirements](shared-limits-drivers). To learn more, see [M0 Cluster Limits](atlas-free-tier).

sections.heading: Commands
sections.body:
Not all commands are supported on all tiers, and Atlas supports some
commands only on Free clusters and Flex clusters.

- Some commands have [limits or are unsupported](free-tier-command-limitations)
  on Free clusters and Flex clusters. To learn more about these unsupported commands, refer to
  [Unsupported Commands in Atlas](unsupported-commands).

- Some commands have [limits on M10+ clusters](limited-commands-paid),
  or are [unsupported on M10+ clusters](unsupported-commands-paid). To
  learn more about these commands, refer to [Commands Available in Flex and Free clusters](free-tier-commands).
